rulings.Case Title: INDEPENDENT BANK, A MICHIGAN CORPORATION VS. ALAN M. ZNEIMER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2017 Line 1. PLAINTIFF INDEPENDENT BANK's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES GRANTED in the amount of $38,697.50.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXIOS DIMITRIOU VS. ANDREW DIMITRIOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2017 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREW DIMITRIOU's Motion To Vacate Dismissal DENIED. While it is not clear why the case needs to be active in order for the parties to complete the terms of the settlement agreement (Reply 4:8-9), based on the statements made in the moving papers the Court will assume that Defendant is seeking to vacate the dismissal in order to file a motion to enforce the settlement agreement with a CCP 664.6 provision. However, it is not necessary to vacate the dismissal for Defendant to file such motion. Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429; Hernandez v. Board of Education (2004) 126 Cal.App.4th 1161.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2017 line 5. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of all elements of the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action, including (1) existence of a fiduciary duty created by contract, conduct, sub agency, etc.; and (2) damages proximately caused by that breach. The damages currently alleged in paragraph 39 of the operative complaint are conclusory and appear to sound in fraud as the only factual support for "coercion" found elsewhere in the Fourth Amended Complaint is based on misrepresentation and concealment, i.e. the gravamen on the complete cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, as currently alleged, sounds in fraud.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT T.C. TAI VS. JOHN M. SANGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Waterview Development Llc To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2017 line 6. INTERVENOR WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT, LLC's Motion Of Waterview Development Llc To Intervene DENIED, moot. Judgment against Tai entered on July 3, 2017, i.e. there are no claims intervenor can join as requested in the proposed complaint in intervention at 5:10. = (501/REQ)
